title: dagr.event-stream@1
description: The C19 event stream: an append-only sequence of single-line JSON records (JSON Lines), one object per line. Every record carries the T0.6/C19 header (run identity, schema version, gapless strictly-increasing sequence, informational wall-clock stamp, authoritative monotonic offset) plus an event body keyed by `kind`. Published by T39 (ticket 050) against the T4 serialization ADR (017). Schema evolution is additive-only within a version (T0.10): no object sets additionalProperties:false, so unknown future fields validate and readers ignore them.

schema_version.description: Self-identifying schema version (T4 §3): the `dagr.event-stream` name disambiguates this record from co-located graph/run artifacts; `@1` is the single major integer.

run_id.description: The run identity every record carries, so a concatenated multi-run stream partitions safely (C19). A UUIDv7 string, operator-overridable.

seq.description: Gapless, strictly-increasing sequence number within a run (C19). A non-negative integer.

wall.description: Informational wall-clock stamp. Never used for durations (offsets are authoritative).

offset_ns.description: Authoritative monotonic offset from run start, in nanoseconds. Durations are computed from offsets, never wall clocks (C19). Required and non-negative.

kind.description: The event kind. The closed set covers every state-transition and lifecycle event (arch.md C19 + Vocabulary).

header.description: Every run-artifact header field KNOWN AT START (C19): everything but the two end-only fields (overall outcome and summary), so a stream that ends one event later still identifies its run completely.

captured_environment.description: Allowlisted captured environment values only (default allowlist empty). Each value is a captured scalar (string/number/boolean), never an unbounded nested structure — the schema does not sanction unbounded environment capture (C22).

not.description: The two end-only fields must NOT appear at start: the run-started event predates the overall outcome and the summary. Expressed as a `not` on their presence (rather than additionalProperties:false) so future additive header fields still validate (T0.10).

then.description: A zombie-at-exit event references the attempt whose thread was left behind (C14). It is an EVENT record, never a terminal-state transition — it carries no terminal `status`.
